title: Sparse Attention
description: An attention variant that computes scores only for a subset of token pairs instead of every query-key combination.
openingSummary: Dense attention scores every query against every key, so compute and memory grow with the square of sequence length; sparse attention applies a mask or pattern that skips most token pairs and computes attention only where the pattern allows, trading full connectivity for lower cost on long contexts.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Sparse attention is an attention variant that restricts which query-key pairs participate in the softmax. Instead of a full n-by-n attention matrix, only entries allowed by a sparsity pattern receive non-zero weight.

whatItOptimizes.title: What It Optimizes
whatItOptimizes.body: Sparse attention targets attention compute, memory bandwidth for score materialization, and the cost of serving long sequences when dense all-pairs attention would dominate runtime.

practicalBenefit.title: Practical Benefit
practicalBenefit.body: Teams adopt sparse attention when they need long-context modeling but cannot afford dense quadratic attention. Skipping disallowed pairs reduces multiply-accumulate work and the memory traffic needed to form and store full attention maps.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: Each query position attends only to keys permitted by a sparsity mask or fixed pattern. Disallowed pairs are zeroed before softmax normalization, so the model spends compute on selected connections rather than the full token grid.

mathOrComputeSchema.title: Math Or Compute Schema
mathOrComputeSchema.body: With sequence length n and sparsity mask M, sparse attention applies M to the score matrix before softmax. The formulas below contrast dense multi-head attention against masked sparse attention that limits which pairs receive weight.

comparedToNearbyModules.title: Compared To Nearby Modules
comparedToNearbyModules.body: Compared with multi-head attention, sparse attention skips most token pairs rather than scoring all of them. Multi-query and grouped-query attention remain dense over allowed positions but reduce KV head count; sparse attention changes which positions connect at all.

exampleArchitectures.title: Example Architectures
exampleArchitectures.body: Sparse attention appears in long-context decoder models that combine local windows, block patterns, or learned sparsity with standard projection layers.

limitationsAndTradeoffs.title: Limitations And Tradeoffs
limitationsAndTradeoffs.body: Restricting connectivity can miss long-range dependencies that dense attention would capture. Pattern choice and mask quality strongly affect what information flows across the sequence.

whyItStillMatters.title: Why It Still Matters
whyItStillMatters.body: As sequence lengths grow, quadratic attention cost motivates sparsity patterns that preserve useful signal while avoiding a full n-by-n score matrix on every layer.

computeFlow.alt: Dense multi-head attention versus sparse attention pair selection
computeFlow.caption: Toggle MHA and sparse attention to compare all-pairs scoring against mask-limited pair selection on one canvas

comparisonTable.caption: How sparse attention compares with nearby attention variants

sparse.computeScaling: Subquadratic when sparsity fraction is fixed
sparse.globalReach: Depends on sparsity pattern; often local or block-structured

mha.attentionConnectivity: All n-by-n query-key pairs per head
mha.globalReach: Any position can attend to any other position

mqa.attentionConnectivity: All n-by-n pairs with shared KV heads
mqa.computeScaling: Quadratic O(n²) with fewer KV projections
mqa.globalReach: Any position can attend to any other position

gqa.attentionConnectivity: All n-by-n pairs with grouped KV heads
gqa.computeScaling: Quadratic O(n²) with reduced KV head count
gqa.globalReach: Any position can attend to any other position

g.definition: Fraction of allowed query-key pairs in the sparsity pattern.

gi.definition: Binary or weighted mask that zeroes disallowed attention pairs.

mhaKvHeads.label: H KV head pairs (1:1 with queries)

mhaOneToOne.label: Each query attends to every key position

sparseSelectedPairs.label: Compute attention on selected query-key pairs
